items.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
items.summary: Google's new offline-first dictation app uses Gemma AI models to take on the apps like Wispr Flow.

items.title: I can’t help rooting for tiny open source AI model maker Arcee
items.summary: Arcee is a tiny 26-person U.S. startup that built a high-performing, massive, open source LLM. And it's gaining popularity with OpenClaw users.

items.title: VC Eclipse has a new $1.3B fund to back — and build — ‘physical AI’ startups
items.summary: Eclipse will put some of that money towards incubating, or building startups.

items.title: Iranian hackers are targeting American critical infrastructure, US agencies warn
items.summary: A joint FBI, NSA, and CISA advisory warns that Iranian hackers have "escalated" their tactics in response to the ongoing U.S.-Israel war with Iran.

items.title: Firmus, the ‘Southgate’ AI data center builder backed by Nvidia, hits $5.5B valuation
items.summary: Nvidia-backed Asia AI data center provider Firmus has now raised $1.35 billion in six months.

items.title: Intel signs on to Elon Musk’s Terafab chips project
items.summary: Intel will join SpaceX and Tesla in an effort to build a new U.S. semiconductor factory in Texas, although the scope of its contributions are unclear.

items.title: Anthropic debuts preview of powerful new AI model Mythos in new cybersecurity initiative
items.summary: The new model will be used by a small number of high-profile companies to engage in defensive cybersecurity work.

items.title: Apple’s foldable iPhone is on track to launch in September, report says
items.summary: The news follows a report from Nikkei Asia on Tuesday that raised concerns the company’s foldable iPhone could be delayed due to challenges during the phone’s engineering test phase.

items.title: Russian government hackers broke into thousands of home routers to steal passwords
items.summary: Fancy Bear, also known as APT28, has taken over thousands of residential home routers to steal passwords and authentication tokens in a wide-ranging espionage operation.

items.title: Chrome finally adds a better way to deal with too many open tabs
items.summary: Google Chrome's latest update brings vertical tabs and a cleaner Reading Mode to help manage cluttered browsing.

items.title: Hate jump scares? This app warns you before they happen
items.summary: The app uses iPhone Live Activities to alert you when scary scenes are about to happen, while also tracking what to watch and where to stream it.

items.title: Uber is the latest to be won over by Amazon’s AI chips
items.summary: Uber is expanding its AWS contract to run more of its ride-sharing features on Amazon's chips. This is a thumb-of-the nose at Oracle and Google.

items.title: Anthropic ups compute deal with Google and Broadcom amid skyrocketing demand
items.summary: Anthropic bulked up its compute deal with Google and Broadcom as the company has seen its run-rate revenue surge to $30 billion.

items.title: Google Maps can now write captions for your photos using AI
items.summary: Google is rolling out new features to make it easier for users to contribute local knowledge to Maps. Most notably, Gemini can now create captions when users are looking to share a photo or video about a place.

items.title: A teenage Minecraft YouTuber raised $1,234,567 for a meme prediction market called Giggles. It broke me.
items.summary: Justin Jin’s company Giggles – which he describes as “putting a trading app and TikTok together” – started as a joke.

items.title: 4 days left to save close to $500 on TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 passes
items.summary: Four days left to save up to $482 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket. These low rates will disappear on April 10 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register now.

items.title: Trump administration plans to cut cybersecurity agency’s budget by $700 million
items.summary: The budget proposal would force CISA to operate with a significantly lower budget than previous years, citing the government's claims that the election misinformation programs were used to "target the President."

items.title: Why a former AirPods engineer is now building heat pumps
items.summary: Merino Energy has simplified the heat pump dramatically in a quest to make them cheaper and easier to install.

items.title: The AI gold rush is pulling private wealth into riskier, earlier bets
items.summary: On a recent episode of Equity, we  talked to Arena Private Wealth to explore a growing trend: family offices bypassing VCs to gain direct exposure to AI startups, turning them from passive investors into active participants.

items.title: Adobe launches Acrobat-based Student Spaces, a free AI-powered study tool for students
items.summary: Adobe Acrobat Student Spaces is a free tool for students to use AI and create different kinds of study material from documents.
